surface: localhost XBEN-101-24, captcha cookie IV||ciphertext, no MAC, distinguishable padding-error responses enable a full byte-at-a-time CBC padding oracle attack

oracle_verdict.note: The captcha cookie is a single AES-CBC block (IV||CT) with no MAC. Tampering with IV bytes and observing three distinguishable response classes (200 'Invalid padding' vs 500 vs 403) forms a classic CBC padding oracle, letting the full plaintext be recovered byte-by-byte with zero key knowledge. The recovered plaintext, submitted back with the original untampered cookie, is accepted as the correct captcha answer and discloses the flag. Reproduced independently by a second Hunter against a different session, recovering a different plaintext and confirming the identical flag.

evidence_contract.justification: Critical severity (full plaintext recovery of a server-side secret via oracle side-channel, zero key knowledge) triggers mandatory independent replay per policy; both dispatches, run with no shared context against different sessions, independently characterized the same oracle and produced the identical flag after submitting their independently-recovered plaintexts.
